notes:
Owner

Starting my career selling cars at 19 while in college, I quickly realized my knack for understanding people and building connections. With a Bachelor's in Marketing and a Master's in Ethical Strategic Leadership from the University of Central Missouri, I expected these degrees to open doors easily. Ironically, I found myself overqualified and facing more hurdles than anticipated, which taught me resilience and adaptability. My sales journey has been diverse, from winning national sales competitions in college to becoming the youngest territory manager in school photography sales. After relocating to Springfield, MO, I turned a struggling territory profitable within my first year, fueling my passion for transforming challenges into opportunities. In 2019, I transitioned to financial advising with Edward Jones, tripling the size of a small book of business within 11 months. The COVID-19 pandemic posed unforeseen challenges, particularly in maintaining client relationships. This led me to pivot to health insurance for a few years until I found my true calling in real estate and investment. My interest in real estate began in 2015 through the Bigger Pockets podcast and books. I bought my first duplex in 2016, living in one side while renting out the other. Despite a tumultuous 2018—marked by a move, job change, and divorce—I emerged stronger, selling my properties to meet financial obligations and focusing on rebuilding. Now, as the Vice President of Investor Relations at Ingram Capital, I bring my diverse experiences together. Partnering with Bryant Dawson, whom I met through Bigger Pockets, we focus on acquiring car washes, multifamily properties, shopping centers, and other cash-flowing businesses, ensuring robust returns for our investors. In addition to my role at Ingram Capital, I co-founded Contractors, Closers, and Connections (CCC) in Kansas City and Northwest Arkansas, creating meaningful, results-driven connections. Currently, I'm writing a book on using self-directed IRAs to invest in commercial real estate, aiming to empower others with the knowledge to achieve financial independence. Having started from scratch twice, I understand the value of perseverance and smart investing. I'm passionate about helping others achieve financial freedom through strategic investments and lasting partnerships.

notes:
Retired!

Providing excellent service since 1995, Furniture Medic by WoodLord Restorations, located in Chestnut Ridge, NY, is owned by a 25 year veteran of the New York City Police Department. We serve residential and commercial customers in Rockland & Westchester counties in NY and Bergen & Essex counties in NJ and surrounding areas. Call us to see if we serve your area! Our phones are answered 24/7 by a live voice....we want your business! We perform punch-list and restoration services on all wood furniture, cabinetry, fixtures and appointments. We offer full upholstery services and a comprehensive fabric selection as well as leather restoration. The products we utilize include water-based, lower odor finishing products which can meet LEED and other green certification requirements. Our skilled technicians arrive in uniform and driving a marked vehicle. Our goal is complete customer satisfaction. We provide service to commercial customers, especially hotels, golf and country clubs, as well as residential customers. Our specialty is serving the insurance industry by restoring items to their pre-loss condition while showing improved accuracies on the bottom line. Mitigation companies and insureds love our "white glove" service! Some of the most popular services we perform include: Touch-ups Mechanical or structural repairs Precision Repairs Punch List completion Repairing and reglueing loose joints Regularly scheduled maintenance visits Fire and water damaged wood restoration EPOs: Emergency Pull-Out for built-ins (saves cabinets/bookcases/etc.) Replacement and fabrication of broken or missing parts Deluxing (machine buffing for desks, tables and conference tables) Restoration (restoring finish, with or without color change) Refinishing (traditional refinishing for badly worn wood surfaces) Please visit our website at www.woodlordrestorations.com or call our office to see what Furniture Medic can do for you!

notes:
Owner/President

I moved to the US in 1987 and quickly ran out of money. To make ends meet, I began painting. I opened my own company, Rainbow Colors Inc. (later to be known as RCI Painting), and was the very first painter for my own company. At first, I set my prices low, but as things got busier, my workload increased significantly. Little by little, I added another helper, and another one, and another one, and today, after 36 years (2023), we are considered to be one of the largest commercial painting companies in South Florida. In December 2022, Benjamin Moore Paints representatives invited me to lunch, handed me a nice rebate check, and told me that my company is their #1 buyer in South Florida. Always true to my word, and all the hard work, commitment, and "never give up" attitude have paid off. I am grateful for the opportunities I've received in this life, and I never take things for granted.

Go Green isn’t just our name; it’s the way we do business. Sustainability is one of our top priorities. From recycling all scrap metal, offering the most efficient systems on the market, recovering harmful refrigerants from old system, and recycling all unused refrigerant to offering paperless options, we recognize that every bit counts when it comes to being responsible stewards to our planet.

Dependability and Accountability:

I graduated from Concordia University of Ann Arbor with a degree in Business Management and a 3.96 cumulative GPA. As a management major, I am always looking for ways to improve the culture of whatever organization I am involved with. Since April of 2017, I have helped grow a small landscaping company from only 4 employees, to over 20. We started by mowing a few lawns and doing some smaller projects here and there, to now having a consistent maintenance schedule as well as several large scale landscape renovations. I love working in small business as it gives me a chance to work alongside a crew of good people. Being able to manage a smaller number of people while still growing rapidly as a company has helped me understand people, and how I can best expose their strengths for the benefit of the company. I am looking for new opportunities in any size organization where I can bring those similar skillsets to the table. I spent a few months with Edwards Glass Company, a commercial glazier, where I started as a project engineer but quickly moved into a project manager position. I also became very familiar with communication between other project managers both inside and outside of the company. Despite my background being mostly with small business, I am open to any new opportunities to where I can provide value. Whether it be with a smaller company still growing, or a large scale commercial company, I am always looking to take on new challenges and further grow in any way possible.

My role has expanded to encompass many responsibilities typically associated with project management, allowing me to integrate operational oversight with hands-on project execution. I am deeply invested in the success of Tri-Tex Roof Systems and am proud of the integral role I play in driving our company's growth and ensuring the highest standards of service. My passion for building strong client relationships and fostering a collaborative team environment continues to fuel my commitment to our collective success. Outside of work, I enjoy immersing myself in the great outdoors. Whether I'm fishing in the serene lakes and rivers of Texas or searching for arrowheads that tell tales of history, these hobbies provide me with relaxation and a sense of connection to nature and the past.

notes:
Product Manager

I’ve always been hands-on and driven to build—whether it’s a house, a team, or a business.

I got my start in construction back in high school, and since then, I’ve worked in various areas, such as HUD projects, renovations, fix-and-flips, REOs, and property preservation. Over the years, I’ve learned how to manage crews, solve problems on the fly, and keep projects moving forward—no matter how tough the challenge.

What I enjoy most about this work is that there’s always something new to learn and improve. The construction world doesn’t sit still and is constantly evolving.

These days, I’m focused on growing, taking on new projects, and meeting people who are also working hard to build something of their own. I’m always open to good conversations, fresh ideas, and new opportunities.

Let’s connect—I’d love to hear what you’re working on!

notes:
Owner / Operator

I’ve spent over 20 years in the swimming pool industry, working in the Arizona sun, handling everything from complex repairs to business operations. I’ve built strong relationships with customers and earned a reputation for solving problems others walk away from. But after years of physical labor, minor health issue, and my age, I had to face a hard truth: I couldn’t keep working the way I always had. That challenge led to opportunity. I began building tools to make my business run without me in the field — and ended up creating a full AI-powered assistant named “Billy.” Billy reads and categorizes emails, automates billing through QuickBooks, remembers customer history, and flags important messages — all with zero code. I built him using real-world problems I faced every day as a pool pro. Since then, I’ve developed: A DIY chatbot that helps homeowners troubleshoot and repair pool issues A smart memory system that simulates long-term context for business tasks Automations that save hours each week on billing, communication, and scheduling I didn’t come from tech — I came from trenches, from pulling engines, and transmissions, to installing filters, and pumps, repairing leaks, and long summers. Now I’m turning the knowledge I earned the hard way into tools other pool professionals and service-based businesses can use to work smarter, stay organized, and protect their health and time.

The Exterior Supply Group is the result of a business started in 1979 by Michael L. Emily. Operating in South Carolina under the name of Greenwood Home Services, he was primarily engaged in residential new construction and renovation. As the need for a local source of distribution in vinyl products became apparent, Mr. Emily expanded his business to include a small office and warehouse for that purpose.

The distribution business rapidly grew and by 1986 the construction division of the company was phased out to focus subsequent efforts exclusively toward developing the wholesale distribution business to its fullest potential.

Yes theres a thousand and one companies that do plumbing, and do it well. But we help people! Helping people with what we’re good at, plumbing! At Rhino Plumbing Solutions our main mission is to offer peace of mind to everyone we come in contact with. We know you have options, so we strive to be the most passionate humans we can be. Director Strategic Partnerships

Static-control flooring is a tiny island in the giant sea of commercial flooring. Yet it’s the least understood, most technically challenging part of the flooring world. My job is to bring clarity to every project, by sharing objective, evidence-based information, helping partners wade through the complexities of testing, floor prep, ESD specs and compliance, and helping them avoid costly mistakes that can affect their wallet or their reputation. 

At StaticWorx, I collaborate closely with architects, designers, and contractors, translating complex technical details into real-world solutions that satisfy mutual objectives. If you’re looking for a partner who listens, genuinely cares, and will always do right by you, let’s connect. 

StaticWorx is responsible for sharing most of the publicly available static-control flooring learning content, the below article is a subject the design community has found most interesting.

From there, we design a complete system that includes the optimal flooring material along with the correct installation method, grounding strategy, footwear protocols, seating, mobile carts, and other elements that influence charge generation and dissipation.

– ShadowFX® SDT carpet tile
– AmeriWorx™ conductive and static-dissipative vinyl tile
– Eclipse™ EC and Eclipse Plus™ conductive rubber
– GroundWorx™ ESD epoxy and coatings

I’m the owner and COO of Olympus Roofing, a company that has been serving the Salt Lake Valley since 1973. Since acquiring the business in 2022, my focus has been on modernizing operations, improving efficiency, and enhancing customer experience. By integrating new technology, refining processes, and investing in employee training, we've been able to create a stronger foundation for long-term growth while maintaining the quality and trust Olympus has been known for over the years. My background in Strategic Management from the Marriott School of Business at BYU, combined with hands-on experience in construction, trucking, landscaping, and automotive mechanics, has given me a unique perspective on both business strategy and skilled trades. Having worked on both sides of the industry, I understand the challenges that come with running a business while also appreciating the technical skills that drive day-to-day operations. This balance helps me identify opportunities for growth and improvement in a way that benefits both employees and customers. I live in the Salt Lake City area with my wife, our new baby, and our dog. Outside of work, I enjoy boating, snowmobiling, competitive shooting, and traveling—always looking for the next adventure.

notes:
VP of Sales and Operations

I was introduced to the flooring world very early, considering my dad worked for Ability ever since I was born. I was very eager to learn as much as I could and start working for the family’s business. From the very first day my dad let me go to work with him, I knew this was going to be long term for me. It all started with riding along side my dad, doing estimates and assisting wherever I was allowed without getting in trouble, whether I was grabbing tools, printing papers or helping the workmen on a project around the office. At that very moment, I knew that this was not just a job that I was embarking on, it was the beginning of a career. I officially joined my father full time in 2016 to spearhead the Sales and Operations department at Ability. Having acquired a Bachelor’s degree from Stetson University in Family Business. After joining Ability Wood Flooring’s I stated a new philosophy for Ability and that was “it’s not about the sale; it’s about the relationship”. I have now made over 1000 new friends from working with Ability since 2016 and I can honestly tell you that I love ever minute of it. Thank you to our wonderful customers for being so great!

I grew up working after school and summers in the family A/C business and learned it pretty well. My Dad pulled out of the company in the mid 90's and my two brothers and I continued to run company for many years. Partnerships don't last forever and I was bought out in 2007. Real Estate was logical, I had done the schooling and received a license earlier that year and I knew I could make it work. So I put my head down and went to work. I specialized in working with bank properties, distressed properties and mostly dealt with investors. My knack for finding the deals brought many other clients my way and I didn't have to do a lot of advertising to stay busy. I started my own brokerage in 2010 and will keep it going as long as my investors keep using my services. My wife Julie is also a real estate agent and runs a good portion of that. I decided earlier this year that I wanted to do more than just real estate. It's hard to build more than a job in real estate no matter how hard you work. Jack Bennett one of the owners of Bennett and Porter has been a friend of mine for several years, he encouraged the switch and has helped me get started. My back ground in construction and real estate has helped me to understand the needs of small businesses and made it easy to talk to business owners. Jack and the others working here have developed some great relationships and programs and we have been able to help most of those we meet with to figure out the correct coverage they need as well as find them the best price on that coverage.

My range stretches from project management to graphic design, from accounting to video editing. Between Hospitality Services, Construction, Indoor Entertainment, and TV & Film, I have over 7 years experience in various forms of accounting. Along the way I have mixed those skills with experiences in Marketing, IT, and Product Management to develop a diverse and useful set of interconnected skills. For 2 years I also served as the Master of Ceremonies for the annual Sky Zone International Convention for 500+ employees and franchisees, along with their biannual all-hands meetings. My latest project required my design, IT, accounting, and strategy skills to successfully convert 150+ domestic franchise Sky Zone indoor parks to a modern gift card platform, while managing a cross-departmental team of 15 people. This conversion led to a 12% growth in gift card sales while the overall year-over-year sales for the network had a 6% decline. Since then I have been fully self-employed working as a handyman, personal assistant, and artist. I have found that whatever problem I face can be resolved, and I look forward to doing my diligence to uncover the most efficient and long lasting resolutions for those problems. I like to see where the cracks are and how to fill and improve them.

Owner/Senior Consultant

Over 35 years in the garage door industry. You could say I started as a boy hanging out at a place called Roberts Overdoors in Derby, Kansas with my father, earning a quarter a night picking up screws, and bolts in the driveway. About 1983 my father started a new, and used garage door company, right behind Roberts. Well nights, and weekends were now gone, for at the ripe old age of 16 I was now starting a career as a garage door tech. At 22 I went to work for Roberts for about a year and a half and still worked for dad nights, and weekends. I feel cursed, and blessed everyday for the knowledge I have for overhead doors. I can solve, or fix most all issues that arise. What I love to sell most is value. and I am not talking dollars, and cents. What I am selling to the customer is my integrity, the doors follow me. I have a drive to always give 110% at everything. Sales is a passion not just a career. When I started at Overhead Door Company, I boosted over the counter sales by over 500%, within a 5 month period by simply starting a wholesale aspect. As a Manager I try my best to learn something new everyday, for in a innovative world, you must research the good, and bad ideas of tomorrow, outweigh the pros and cons and implement them if needed. I am with a company now that I helped start growing in 2017, it is Derby Overhead Company and I will Take it to the Top, for building business is what I do best.

notes:
Roofing Consultant

My career began while attending middle/high school. Working during vacations/holidays in my father’s businesses provided exposure to the manufacture and application of waterproofing membranes and coatings to roofs and walls of buildings. Following graduation in 1995, I worked in my brother’s company, Design Waterproofing Systems, at building sites learning the trade from the bottom up. While there I gained practical experience in the properties and types of waterproofing materials and their various applications. I then became a Contracts Site Manager which also embraced functions such as diagnosing problems and specifying the materials to cure them, measuring the sites and calculating quotations for material and labor on both existing and new buildings. In 2000 I started my own businesses in Cape Town, manufacturing and applying waterproofing materials. Over the past 20 years, I have acquired extensive expertise as to how water ingress occurs and migrates in building substrates, both roofs and walls, and how to combat various problems, including the occurrence of negative pressure in wine cellars and underground basements. I developed specialist knowhow in silicone injection and salt retardant plaster for rising damp, repairs to spalling render and wall cracks, coating of weather facing walls with waterproof emulsions and silicone masonry protection for face-brick and porous cement blocks. notes:
Owner/Certified Dryer Exhaust Technician

I am "A jack of all trades and a master of none, but oftentimes better than a master of one". Throughout my life I have worked hard were ever I worked and played. Moving around has always been the theme my life. Having different jobs have given me a wide expanse of experiences. Even though the positions I held varied, there had been a constant variable with them all, building relationships. Whether it was being a swim coach, sailing school manager a children's shoe sales person or a banker. I have done a lot more than what I have mentioned with the same common theme, helping people realize their potential. In 2010 while trying to figure out the next move I would make in my everlasting search for the perfect job, by the way, there isn't one, I wanted to started a business that would last and be successful. LintFree Dryer Vent, LLC is my baby. I am the owner/operator for 14 year. I have and do love my job, however, there is a time in which one, especially me, has to ask is it time for a new adventure. With more and more people getting into this industry, it has become increasingly hard to keep the market share that I used to have. So I have decided to start looking for something relatively new. People have asked me what I am looking for. Well, I am looking for a position that is interesting, in a industry that I am familiar with, banking, loan originating, perhaps. should be challenging, where there are people involved and that I could make a decent salary doing. Is there a position that would fit me, with my diverse history? I say, there is. I am open to the many possibilities that are out there.

notes:
Sales Specialist

"All the worlds a stage, and all the men and women merely players" - William Shakespeare Performing lit a fire within me. I love telling a good story. Whether I'm acting in a short film or selling a product or service, there is a story to be told. The beginning always starts with a person with a problem, followed by attempts to escape it, failures to do so, then finally discovering a solution that works. No matter where I enter, my goal is to help clients see themselves as the hero of their own story. Framing myself or the brand not as the savior, but as the guide or tool that leads them to their own victory. I started my sales journey in the roofing industry. My father started and owns a successful roofing company in Dallas, TX where I started working almost immediately after graduating college with an oh-so-versatile Fine-Arts Acting degree. I am incredibly proud of this degree, as it was a highly competitive program for which thousands applied and only 14 were accepted. I quickly found that the communication skills, collaborative training, and performance techniques I learned in acting classes were paramount to any product knowledge I had mastered from being raised in the industry. At the end of the day, people want to be heard and valued. Listening is one of the most important aspects of performance, and I soon found the same to be true of sales. In order to form a solution, it's crucial to understand the problem. It's typical for actors and performers to live and think abstractly, but my approach to performance has been one of deep analytics. I can sometimes become obsessed with the question, "why?" I avoid the pits of existential crisis this thinking can lead to, and rather use it as a means to understand motivation. Why do people do what they do? The human condition has always fascinated me, it is as surprising as it is elusive. Being human myself, completely understanding people is impossible, which makes it just that much more fun to try to figure out. It's a true journey, and I've learned to delight in the process.

Experienced and versatile professional with a robust background in both the roofing industry and hospitality management. Currently serving as a Production Manager at Tri-Tex Roof Systems in central Texas with over four years of dedicated service. In this role, I have honed my project management skills, overseeing production schedules, coordinating with teams, and ensuring top-quality results for residential and commercial roofing projects. Prior to my current role, I spent nearly eight years at Brooklyn Heights Pizzeria, where I gained invaluable experience across various positions. As a General Manager for seven months, I was responsible for overall operations, employee management, and customer satisfaction. As an Assistant Manager for four years, I played a key role in staff training, inventory management, and daily operational efficiency. My journey at Brooklyn Heights Pizzeria started as a Bartender for over three years, where I developed excellent customer service skills and a strong work ethic. notes:
Owner

With a background in insurance, mortgage, and real estate, I’ve spent my career helping people make informed decisions about their most valuable assets. I understand the financial and logistical challenges that property owners and managers face when it comes to maintaining their buildings—and I also know how complex the insurance and claims process can be after a storm. That’s why at Tri-Tex Roof Systems, I take a consultative approach to roofing. My focus isn’t just on fixing or replacing a roof—it’s on helping clients protect their properties, understand their options, and make the most strategic decisions possible. Whether you’re navigating an insurance claim, planning for preventative roof maintenance, or simply trying to extend the life of your investment, I’m here to help. At Tri-Tex, we specialize in: -Storm Restoration & Insurance Collaboration Assistance – Helping property owners understand their coverage, work within insurance guidelines, and maximize their claims without unnecessary stress. -Commercial & Residential Roofing – Providing customized solutions that align with both property needs and budget realities. -Preventative Maintenance Plans – Helping property managers and commercial building owners avoid costly repairs through regular roof assessments and preventative care. Why Work With Me? My experience across real estate, mortgage, and insurance allows me to bridge the gap between roofing, finance, and claims processes—giving my clients a competitive edge in protecting their properties. Outside of work, I enjoy spending time with my wife and kids, playing guitar, and making the most of life’s everyday moments. Let’s connect! If you’re a property owner, manager, or industry professional looking for insights into roofing, insurance claims, or preventative maintenance, I’m happy to help.

Dispatcher

My name is Holly Mills, I currently reside in the beautiful small town of Corryton. When I was in High school I didn’t know what I wanted to do. Right after graduation I got a job at the Pilot gas station about 10 minutes from my dads house. It started out as a job as a cashier just a Monday-Friday job. I didn’t know it would be that job that showed me that I was able to advance and become more than just a cashier. I started working my way up and then achieving an assistant management before the age of 21. I eventually decided to leave due to some family members having serious health issues and needed help. I became a bartender for a short period of time at nights to help make a little extra money. I didn’t know I would soon be starting a family of my own. After I had my little girl I got a job in a “call center” starting as a customer service representative, advanced to the retail department within my first year, getting everything flat and stable. After that I advanced to inside sales, not knowing in a few months I would become the lead dispatcher. I strive everyday to make sure that my family comes first, but not only that, to be better today than I was yesterday. I take pride in everything that I do and I want to accomplish my goal of growing and becoming more successful.

HTA takes great pride in the hard work and amazing talent we have that has established us as a highly respected HVAC company on the Island. We specialize in residential homeowners and look forward to serving you.

Founder

My team and I provide HVAC services to customers in residential homes and office buildings, all the way up to power plants. We believe in offering honest solutions that are right for your building, your system, and your budget. I have enjoyed many HVAC positions throughout my career - working in customer homes, on military bases in the marines, working for nation-wide industrial companies, and in power plants. Indoor air isn’t “one size fits all”. What I love most about the HVAC industry is that every challenge is met with a different solution. And to us, our customers are a name and a face - not an address and a job number. I love meeting new people and discussing new ideas. Reach out if you want to talk about air conditioning, food, or soccer! I’d love to see how we can help you achieve your indoor air goals! 125+ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Google Reviews

notes:
President

With over 15 years of experience in home energy evaluations, I have dedicated my career to helping builders and homeowners optimize their home performance. My journey began before 2008, where I advised clients on energy solutions, gaining invaluable insights into the insulation market while collaborating with contractors. Recognizing a gap in quality insulation services, I took the initiative to equip myself with the necessary tools and expertise, launching my own insulation service. Today, Casco Bay Insulation has blossomed, largely through word-of-mouth referrals and partnerships with some of the top home builders in Southern Maine. Our team is composed of passionate professionals who share a commitment to excellence, focusing on delivering high-quality solutions. Providing full building envelope services for new construction and modern existing homes, we pride ourselves on being able to unlock and improve the energy puzzles that high performance homes present. Our range of insulation and air sealing solutions always include dense pack cellulose as our insulation of choice. Let's connect to discuss innovative solutions in energy efficiency and sustainable building practices!

notes:
Project Manager

As a Civil Engineering student at Colorado State University, I have always been fascinated by heavy civil engineering and the process in constructing dams, bridges, highways, and water and utility distribution. I would love the opportunity to add value to your team. I am confident in my ability to contribute as a civil engineer intern with my technical knowledge, relevant industry experience, and well-rounded skillset. As a motivated problem solver and diligent learner, I have developed proficiency in the areas of plan drawings and analysis. During my internship in the Estimating Department at Hilbers Inc., I estimated two projects from start to finish; gathered proposals, blueprints, specifications, and related documents; applied plan designs into the company’s website and sent out invitations to bid for the job; prepared construction budgets, by studying the construction plans, updating specifications, and identifying and projecting costs for each elevation; obtained bids from vendors and subcontractors, by specifying materials, identifying qualified subcontractors, and negotiating prices. In the end, I delivered a comprehensive and cost-effective bid on an aggressive timeline. Civil Engineering requires the technical, communication, and organizational abilities that I have demonstrated during my time at Colorado State and in my work experience. I believe an Internship can help me further my education, by providing me the opportunity to be hands-on in the field. I want to work at the construction sites; getting my hands dirty, monitoring operations, and solving onsite problems. Please review my attached resume which provides additional details of my education and employment experience. I believe my interpersonal and problem-solving abilities will provide an invaluable asset to any team.

notes:
Customer Service Representative

Working in Customer Service whether by phone, email, text or person to person has shown me how to enhance a customers experience. Ive worked hard to make sure at the end of a customers experience they are impressed and will return. Over 8 years of experience I believe open communication is a huge part in being successful. This means with the customers you talk to, to your management and your co workers around you. I work extremely hard to have good communication with the customers because then we are clear on issue and I can resolve it quickly. I love talking to customers because I try to connect with them besides the issue they called in for. I have been through so many different experiences in my life it makes it easier to find something in common with the customer. I think this is important because it shows customers we care about them and value their business and appreciates it. While talking to a customer I make sure they feel heard, understood and validated because in doing this it brings customers back because of the service they received.

Customer Service Representative

I like to keep things light around the office, yet I am able to work under pressure. My belief is friends in the workplace is what keeps morale and productivity high. Because of the Air Force, I'm very easy-going in general attitude and adaptable about whatever last-minute project or situation may arise. As a result, I can stay flexible since nothing stays the same on the job. My work speaks for itself: Every customer I have dealt with enjoys that I helped them and treated them like a person and every team member comes to me for aid because I'm a hands-on teacher and walk the talk with my open door policy. Don't let my silly personality fool you! I give whatever company I work for 100% and NOTHING less. Due to my military experience, I have been trained to do anything and everything at maximum output with the highest quality result possible. I have excellent people skills and am able to work with tense situations. A caring ear goes a long way to avoid escalation of issues that don't need to be escalated in the first place. This includes both customers and team members. Because I've hit my peak as a customer support agent, I'd love to further my computer knowledge working in any computer-related industry, ideally continuing as a data analyst. I'm always eager to expand my knowledge and increase my skills. Learning new things is a fun challenge for me and I actually get really excited and motivated when I get to do something new or particularly challenging that others don't wanna do. Some people need coffee, I need a something interesting to do! I'm looking for something remote because of many reasons (I have cats, my sister has babies and needs a baby sitter, my parents are elderly...) and it's easier for me to be nearby in case something happens. My passion is in video games and I don't make that a secret. I dream of making my own indie video game and I already have a demo out that's constantly evolving and growing. I'm also a novel writer on Wattpad in my free time and I have multiple stories in the process of being created. Overall, writing is a hobby of mine and whether be it in games or books, I LOVE anything with a great story. If there's anything you want to know, don't hesitate to reach out. I'm an open book and not afraid to answer questions!

A Skilled Nursing Assistant is observant, compassionate, patient, engaged, motivated, sharp, and above all caring. So many lives on a daily basis depend on the care and treatment that nurses provide, which is why I chose to pursue this career. Throughout the years I have honed these skills to be the best caregiver I can be, however, there is one skill that I lack, selling myself. So instead I will provide a list of some things about me and my work. 1) I have always been told that I am a personable person. A vital skill that all Nursing Assistants need is the ability to engage with their patients. Every job I have had in the past has helped me develop this skill to its fullest extent. Working as a sales representative for a construction company has especially helped with this. Even though construction and nursing are on opposite sides of the job spectrum, both jobs require understanding and engagement with people. 2) Before my first Nursing Assistant position I was already caring for a large group of people, and by that I mean the eight siblings that I am blessed with! Growing up as one of the oldest kids in the family is probably the biggest training excercises I have had in the steps to becoming a nurse. 3) I knew that nursing was what I wanted to do since I was a kid and I have stuck with it. My weekends and summers in high school were spent volunteering at local nursing facilitees. 4) What sets me apart from others is the fact that I would do this job for free. Nursing isn't just a paycheck for me, it is a passion.
